title: A Weekend Trip to the Market
summary: A woman describes her Saturday morning visit to the local farmers' market, where she buys fresh vegetables, meets a friendly stallholder, and enjoys the lively atmosphere.
body: Every Saturday morning I go to the farmers' market near my house. I usually leave home at nine o'clock so I can arrive before the crowds. The market has many stalls selling fresh vegetables, fruit, bread, and flowers. Last Saturday I bought a large bag of carrots, some tomatoes, and a loaf of sourdough bread. The bread smelled wonderful. I also stopped at a flower stall and bought a small bunch of yellow sunflowers for my kitchen table. The stallholder was very friendly. She told me that the sunflowers came from a farm just outside the city. I chatted with her for a few minutes and she gave me a free bunch of herbs because I was a regular customer. I paid for everything with cash and put it all in my big canvas bag. On the way home I stopped at a small café and had a cup of tea. It was a lovely morning and I felt very happy.

annotations.meaningVi: chợ nông sản (nơi nông dân bán sản phẩm trực tiếp)
annotations.exampleEn: We buy fresh eggs and vegetables at the farmers' market every weekend.
She always arrives early to get a good seat before the crowds.
He bought some cheese from a stall at the market.

annotations.meaningVi: ổ bánh mì chua (làm từ men tự nhiên)
annotations.exampleEn: She sliced the loaf of sourdough bread for breakfast.
He gave her a bunch of roses on her birthday.
The sunflowers in the garden grew very tall this summer.

annotations.meaningVi: người bán hàng tại quầy chợ
annotations.exampleEn: The stallholder offered us a free sample of cheese.

annotations.meaningVi: khách hàng quen, khách hàng thường xuyên
annotations.exampleEn: As a regular customer, she always gets a small discount.

annotations.meaningVi: túi vải bố (thường dùng để mua sắm)
annotations.exampleEn: I always bring my canvas bag to the market to avoid using plastic.
On the way home she stopped to buy a newspaper.
They live just outside the city, about twenty minutes by train.

grammarNotes.title: Simple past tense for completed actions: 'I bought', 'she told', 'I stopped'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Thì quá khứ đơn (động từ dạng V2 hoặc thêm -ed) dùng để mô tả các hành động đã hoàn thành tại một thời điểm cụ thể trong quá khứ. Ví dụ: 'Last Saturday I bought a large bag of carrots.' — Thứ bảy tuần trước tôi đã mua một túi cà rốt lớn. 'She told me that the sunflowers came from a farm.' — Cô ấy nói với tôi rằng hoa hướng dương đến từ một trang trại. Hành động đã kết thúc và không còn tiếp diễn.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the simple past tense to describe actions that were completed at a specific time in the past. Regular verbs take -ed (stopped, chatted, smelled); irregular verbs change form (bought, told, gave, felt, had). 'Last Saturday I bought a large bag of carrots.' The action is finished. Contrast with present: 'Every Saturday I go to the market' (habit, present simple) versus 'Last Saturday I bought...' (specific past event).

grammarNotes.title: Frequency adverbs: 'usually', 'always', 'every'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Usually' (thường thường), 'always' (luôn luôn), và 'every + time word' (mỗi...) được dùng để nói về thói quen hoặc hành động lặp đi lặp lại. Chúng thường đứng trước động từ chính hoặc sau động từ 'to be'. Ví dụ: 'I usually leave home at nine o'clock.' — Tôi thường ra khỏi nhà lúc chín giờ. 'Every Saturday morning I go to the farmers' market.' — Mỗi sáng thứ bảy tôi đi chợ nông sản.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Frequency adverbs and expressions like 'usually', 'always', and 'every Saturday' describe habits or repeated actions in the present. They are used with the present simple tense. 'Usually' and 'always' go before the main verb: 'I usually leave home at nine.' 'Every + time word' goes at the start or end of the sentence: 'Every Saturday morning I go to the market.' These are different from past actions, which use simple past.

translationVi: Mỗi sáng thứ bảy tôi đi đến chợ nông sản gần nhà. Tôi thường rời nhà lúc chín giờ để có thể đến trước khi đông người. Chợ có nhiều quầy hàng bán rau củ tươi, trái cây, bánh mì và hoa. Thứ bảy tuần trước tôi mua một túi cà rốt lớn, vài quả cà chua và một ổ bánh mì chua. Bánh mì có mùi thơm tuyệt vời. Tôi cũng ghé vào một quầy hoa và mua một bó hoa hướng dương vàng nhỏ để trang trí bàn bếp. Người bán hàng rất thân thiện. Cô ấy nói với tôi rằng hoa hướng dương đến từ một trang trại ngay bên ngoài thành phố. Tôi trò chuyện với cô ấy một vài phút và cô ấy tặng tôi một bó rau thơm miễn phí vì tôi là khách hàng quen. Tôi trả tiền mặt cho tất cả mọi thứ và bỏ vào túi vải lớn của mình. Trên đường về nhà tôi ghé vào một quán cà phê nhỏ và uống một tách trà. Đó là một buổi sáng tuyệt vời và tôi cảm thấy rất vui.

questions.prompt: When does the narrator go to the market?
questions.explanation: The passage begins: 'Every Saturday morning I go to the farmers' market.'
questions.hint: The first sentence tells you the day and time.

questions.prompt: What time does she usually leave home?
questions.explanation: 'I usually leave home at nine o'clock.'
questions.hint: She leaves early to beat the crowds.

questions.prompt: Why does she leave home early?
questions.explanation: 'I usually leave home at nine o'clock so I can arrive before the crowds.'
questions.hint: She mentions a specific reason using 'so I can'.

questions.prompt: Which of these did she NOT buy last Saturday?
questions.explanation: She bought carrots, tomatoes, sourdough bread, and sunflowers — not grapes.
questions.hint: Think about what was listed and what was not.

questions.prompt: What did she notice about the bread?
options: It was freshly baked and warm
It was made with wholemeal flour
questions.hint: She uses one of the five senses to describe it.
The colour is mentioned just before the word sunflowers.

questions.prompt: Where did the sunflowers come from?
options: A garden in the city centre
A farm just outside the city
questions.explanation: 'She told me that the sunflowers came from a farm just outside the city.'

questions.prompt: Why did the stallholder give her free herbs?
options: Because she spent a lot of money
Because she was a regular customer
questions.explanation: 'she gave me a free bunch of herbs because I was a regular customer.'

questions.prompt: How did she pay for her shopping?
questions.hint: She mentions the payment method directly.

questions.prompt: What did she carry her shopping in?
questions.explanation: 'I put it all in my big canvas bag.'
questions.hint: She describes the bag by its material.

questions.prompt: What did she do on the way home?
options: She stopped at a small café for tea
questions.explanation: 'On the way home I stopped at a small café and had a cup of tea.'
questions.hint: She made one stop before reaching home.

questions.prompt: How did she feel at the end of the morning?
questions.explanation: 'It was a lovely morning and I felt very happy.'
questions.hint: The final sentence describes her mood.

cloze.template: Every Saturday morning I go to the farmers' {{b1}} near my house. I usually leave home at nine o'clock so I can arrive before the {{b2}}. The market has many {{b3}} selling fresh vegetables, fruit, bread, and flowers. Last Saturday I bought a large bag of carrots, some tomatoes, and a {{b4}} of sourdough bread. The bread {{b5}} wonderful. I also stopped at a flower stall and bought a small bunch of yellow {{b6}} for my kitchen table. The stallholder was very {{b7}}. She told me that the sunflowers came from a farm just {{b8}} the city. I chatted with her for a few minutes and she gave me a free bunch of {{b9}} because I was a regular {{b10}}. I paid for everything with {{b11}} and put it all in my big canvas bag. On the way home I stopped at a small café and had a cup of {{b12}}.

blanks.explanation: 'market' — a farmers' market is an outdoor place to buy fresh produce.
'crowds' — she leaves early to arrive before the large number of people.
'stalls' — individual selling points at a market are called stalls.
'loaf' — bread comes in a loaf; a loaf of bread is the standard expression.
'smelled' — 'the bread smelled wonderful' uses a sensory verb.
'sunflowers' — she specifically bought yellow sunflowers for her kitchen table.
'friendly' — the passage says 'The stallholder was very friendly.'
'outside' — 'just outside the city' means very close to the city but not in it.
'herbs' — the stallholder gave her a free bunch of herbs.
'customer' — a regular customer is someone who shops there frequently.
'cash' — she paid with cash, meaning physical money.
'tea' — she stopped at a café and had a cup of tea on the way home.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator buys fresh food at a local farmers' market every week. Some people prefer to shop at large supermarkets because it is more convenient. What are the advantages and disadvantages of buying food at a farmers' market compared to a supermarket? Which do you prefer and why?
